metadata.name: celus-nigiri
metadata.summary: Library for downloading and parsing counter-like data.

classes.name: celus_nigiri.record.Identifiers
documentation:
Because of dataclass it should have constant memory footprint and
memory consumption should be lower as well when it contains at last one item
compared to ordinary dict.

For comaptiblity reasons it should work as a dict for some operations.
TODO since python3.10 there is `slot=True` attr which can really
make this simpler (no need to override init anymore and define __slots__)

functions.name: celus_nigiri.utils.parse_counter_month
documentation:
Returns a date object extracted from date formatted according to the specification of COUNTER 5
i. e. Mmm-YYYY. It also allows Mmm-YY to be more flexible
\:param text:
\:return:

functions.name: celus_nigiri.utils.parse_date_fuzzy
documentation: Uses dateutil.parser to try to parse a date in US format.

documentation: Detect which csv dialect is most appropriate for counter-like data

functions.name: celus_nigiri.csv_detect.histogram_score
documentation: Calculate the score of the histogram for comprisons

modules.name: celus_nigiri.celus
documentation: Celus format for non-counter data

functions.name: celus_nigiri.celus.col_name_to_month
documentation:
```pycon
>>> col_name_to_month('Jan 2019')
datetime.date(2019, 1, 1)
>>> col_name_to_month('2019-02')
datetime.date(2019, 2, 1)
>>> col_name_to_month('prase') is None
True
```

classes.name: celus_nigiri.client.Sushi5Client

methods.name: celus_nigiri.client.Sushi5Client._encode_date
documentation:
```pycon
>>> Sushi5Client._encode_date('2018-02-30')
'2018-02'
>>> Sushi5Client._encode_date(datetime(2018, 7, 6, 12, 25, 30))
'2018-07'
```

documentation: Prepare download url of a sushi server

methods.name: celus_nigiri.client.Sushi5Client.make_download_params
documentation: Prepare download params which are used to query sushi server

methods.name: celus_nigiri.client.Sushi5Client.fetch_report_data
documentation:
Makes a request for the data, stores the resulting data into `dump_file` and returns
the response object for further inspection
\:param report_type:
\:param begin_date:
\:param end_date:
\:param dump_file: where to put file output
\:param params:
\:return:

methods.name: celus_nigiri.client.Sushi5Client.validate_data
documentation:
Checks that the parsed erorrs and warings are not fatal.

: If so, it will raise SushiException

```
param errors

list of errors

param warnings

list of warnings

return

```
Client for SUSHI and COUNTER 5 protocol
